assessment.body: claudecode renders interactive trust/permission dialogs only in its TUI (the folder-trust prompt on launch, and per-tool permission prompts). That blocked state never reaches the transcript, and the claudecode hook is the only transcript-path signal for it — unavailable when the hook is not installed (hooks hard-code port 7837). Read-back (issue #732) captures the rendered screen through the same backend the control path targets — tmux capture-pane / kitty get-text, resolved from session.Launcher — recognizes the dialog (backchannel.DetectUI), and TerminalObserver fuses it to a waiting state via the SessionDetector's single writer. It is gated by the same chain as the write path (backchannel toggle -> control consent -> readable backend) and de-duplicated against the hook (no transition when the session is already waiting). Multiplexer/kitty-only: a session hosted only by plain iTerm2/Terminal.app is not readable and surfaces no terminal-derived signal. Proven against a real tmux pane by TestBackchannelObserveE2E_Local, with unit coverage for detection, fusion (rising/clearing edges, no double-count), and the gate.

sources.note: tmux capture-pane / kitty get-text reader keyed off Launcher
DetectUI trust-dialog matcher (snapshot-only, no VT100 emulator)
polls gated+readable sessions, forwards UI edges
handleTerminalUISignal fuses the edge to waiting on the single writer
